site.title: The Slow Table
site.description: Personal recipe book with an AI nutritionist

header.tagline: Your kitchen · your cookbook · your AI nutritionist

home.heroDescriptor: Your place for the recipes you love. From social media, from mom, from your own experiments — all in one book, private and always at hand. AI calculates nutrition, generates cover art, and fills in the card from any URL.
home.heroCtaAddRecipe: Add a recipe to your book →

heroFacts.l: calories are real data, not an AI guess

home.heroCaptionTitle: Croissant with brie, plum and pistachio cream
home.editorP1: This is my thirteenth year in Prague. I left with a single suitcase and the fear that I’d never recreate the smell of home. So I began collecting recipes — typing them into the notes on my phone, photographing the pages of my mother’s notebook, coaxing my grandmother into explaining how she bakes that sorrel pie, saving finds from social media and rewriting them to fit my family. In time, people started asking me to “send the recipe.” This book is what it all became: a collection of family recipes, and my own, gathered along the way.
home.editorP2: No “healthy weight loss” here, no “boosted metabolism,” none of the advice you’ve heard a million times. Here is what I cook on weekdays and weekends: beautiful, delicious dishes for myself, the people I love, and guests — for when you want comfort, flavour, and the smell of something good. With stories. With an AI nutritionist that counts calories precisely, through USDA. And with a place where you can collect your own recipes.
home.editorSign: — Daria Bobina, editor. Prague, July MMXXVI.
home.pullQuote: Food is not fuel. It’s memories, love and care.
home.emptySubtitle: The most loved dishes will appear here soon — the ones made with soul and warmth.
home.statsTitle: Why this isn’t just another recipe collection.

stats.t: Nutrition accuracy — we don't make up calories, we use real <usda>USDA</usda> data.
Recipes free — start your book without a card.
Premium — unlimited book, import recipes by link, and unlimited AI nutrition.
Lifetime seats for the first readers. One payment — forever.

home.subLede: Free gives you the book and nutrition calculations to get started. Premium removes the caps: unlimited AI nutrition, import recipes from any site, and an unlimited personal book.
home.subLifetimeLede: One payment — Premium forever + 10 AI cover images as a gift. Closes once all 50 seats are filled.
home.demoTitleAccent: to a page in your book.
home.demoLede: Paste a recipe link — and The Slow Table fills in the ingredients, works out exact nutrition, and creates a cover.
home.demoDescription: A warm croissant, melting brie and sweet plum with pistachio. A five-minute breakfast that feels like a small celebration.
home.demoNote: This is how it works in your book too: ingredients, exact USDA-based nutrition, and a cover — all from one link. The image in the reel is real.

pricing.downgradeConfirmText: AI access (link, covers, nutrition) turns off right away. Recipes and covers you already saved stay as they are. If you have more than 15 recipes, you won't be able to create new ones until you delete some or go back to Premium.

premium.title: Welcome to
premium.body: Your subscription is active. You now have access to AI nutrition, AI covers, and recipe import by URL. If your plan hasn’t updated yet, refresh the dashboard in a moment.

lifetime.title: Welcome to
lifetime.body: One payment — Premium forever. AI nutrition, AI covers, and recipe import are yours without a subscription. Plus 10 AI cover credits have been added to your account.

pack.title: Cover pack
pack.body: Your credits have been added. Open any recipe in your book and generate covers — each cover uses one credit from the pack.

pricing.testimonialQuote: I paid for Notion templates and dropped them within a week. This is the first cooking subscription I’m not embarrassed about.
faq: Any EU/UK/US cards via Paddle. Visa/Mastercard from Kazakhstan, Armenia, Georgia, Israel and the UAE work. Cards issued in Russia don’t yet.
What if I subscribe and it’s not for me?

That’s exactly what the free plan is for — try the book and recipes with no card added and decide at your own pace. You can cancel the subscription in one click anytime; Lifetime is a one-time, considered purchase.
Does the AI really count accurately?

We don't ask AI to guess calories. Ingredients are matched against an open food database maintained by US government scientists (USDA FoodData Central) — an open source used by nutritionists worldwide. If an ingredient isn't found, we'll ask you instead of making it up.
What happens to my recipes if I switch to Free?

All your recipes and covers stay as they are — they're yours. AI features (nutrition, cover generation, adding by link) will turn off. If you have more than 15 recipes, you won't be able to add new ones until you delete some or come back to Premium. The Free limit is 15 recipes.
pricing.finalLede: Free gives you the book. Premium lifts the ceilings. Lifetime — for the first fifty. Choose.

nutrition.title: Nutrition
nutrition.manualMethod: Calories and macros provided by the recipe's author.

resolve.notFoundQuestion: «{name}» — not in our database. What to count?
resolve.noExactMatch: No exact match in the USDA database. Here are the closest options — pick one, search for your own, or leave it out.
resolve.searchAll: Full database — {count} ingredients. Start typing to filter.
resolve.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
resolve.timeout: AI is taking too long. Please try again in a minute.

unmatched.title: Not found in our database ({count, plural, one {# ingredient} other {# ingredients}})
unmatched.subtitle: These ingredients weren't included in the calculation. Tell us what to do — your choice will be remembered for future recipes.
unmatched.noSuggestions: No similar products found. Pick one manually or skip from the calculation.
unmatched.error: Failed to save your choice. Please try again.
unmatched.timeout: AI is taking too long to respond. Please try again in a minute.

fuzzy.title: {count, plural, one {# substitution — please review} other {# substitutions — please review}}
fuzzy.subtitle: AI matched similar products from our database. Confirm or swap — we'll remember your choice for future recipes.
fuzzy.allDone: All substitutions confirmed — thank you! Figures updated.
fuzzy.error: Failed to save. Please try again.
fuzzy.timeout: AI is taking too long to respond. Please try again in a minute.

myRecipes.title: My Cookbook
myRecipes.emptyHint: Create your first — only you can see it.
myRecipes.savedEmptyHint: Tap the heart on a recipe card in the catalog and it'll show up here.
myRecipes.emptyLede: Paste a link to a recipe you love, or just list the ingredients — and The Slow Table turns it into a beautiful page with exact AI nutrition.
myRecipes.emptyPrivacyNote: This is your personal book — only you can see it.
myRecipes.emptyMagic1Body: Paste a link from any site — ingredients and steps fill in automatically.
myRecipes.emptyMagic2Body: The AI nutritionist works out calories and macros from your ingredients — no manual entry.
myRecipes.fieldDescriptionPlaceholder: A few words about the dish
myRecipes.fieldNotePlaceholder: A personal story about the dish — where it came from, what it means to you…
myRecipes.ingredientsPlaceholder:
Pumpkin
Ginger
Coconut milk
Onion
Garlic
myRecipes.deleteConfirm: Delete this recipe? This can't be undone.
myRecipes.errStep: Fill in a description for every step
myRecipes.errLimit: Free limit reached ({limit}). Unlimited on Premium.
myRecipes.nutritionHint: The AI nutritionist will calculate accurate nutrition from your ingredients.
myRecipes.nutritionTimeout: AI is taking too long to respond. Please try again in a minute.
myRecipes.nutritionUnresolved: {count, plural, one {# ingredient isn't counted in the nutrition yet} other {# ingredients aren't counted in the nutrition yet}}. Resolve them above — or save as is and refine the figures later.
myRecipes.nutritionManualHint: Enter per-serving nutrition manually — for composite or non-standard dishes.
myRecipes.nutritionManualNote: These numbers are yours, not from USDA. Shown with an 'entered manually' note.
myRecipes.nutritionApprox: {count, plural, one {# ingredient needs a closer look} other {# ingredients need a closer look}}.
myRecipes.nutritionNeedIngredients: Fill in the ingredients to calculate
myRecipes.importHint: Paste a recipe link — the fields fill in automatically. Add the cover and step photos yourself.
myRecipes.importDoneStructured: Done — fields filled from the page's data. Review and save.
myRecipes.importDoneAi: Done — AI parsed the page. Double-check the fields: something may be slightly off.
myRecipes.importPartial: We could only pull a little from the page — the ingredients didn't come through. Review the fields and fill in manually if needed.
myRecipes.importErrTimeout: The site took too long to respond. Try again or use another link.
myRecipes.importErrUnreachable: Couldn't open this link. Check it or try another one.
myRecipes.importErrJsBlocked: This site won't let us grab the recipe automatically. Copy the ingredients and steps in manually.
myRecipes.importErrNotRecipe: No recipe found on that page. Try a direct recipe link or fill it in manually.
myRecipes.importErrGeneric: Couldn't grab the recipe from this link. Try another one or fill it in manually.
myRecipes.modalHint: Enter a name and pick a type — the rest comes next
myRecipes.drinkNoNutrition: Drinks have no nutrition, time or servings — just ingredients and steps.
myRecipes.coverAiHint: Works best once the recipe is filled in: the name, ingredients and steps make the image more accurate.
myRecipes.coverAiDone: Done — you can replace it with a real photo or generate another.
myRecipes.leaveMessage: You've started something but haven't saved it yet. You can stay and finish — or leave, and the draft won't be saved.
myRecipes.confirmCloseMessage: You've started something but haven't saved it yet. Your input will be lost.
